title: Implement property-based tests for all finite field operations
description:
Create a comprehensive property-based test harness using `proptest` that verifies field axioms for any `T: FiniteField` implementation. This harness gates all field implementations — no type is considered correct without passing the axiom tests. ## Axioms to test ### Additive group - **Associativity**: `(a + b) + c == a + (b + c)` - **Commutativity**: `a + b == b + a` - **Identity**: `a + a.zero_like() == a` - **Inverse**: `a + (-a) == a.zero_like()` - **Subtraction consistency**: `a - b + b == a` ### Multiplicative group - **Associativity**: `(a * b) * c == a * (b * c)` - **Commutativity**: `a * b == b * a` - **Identity**: `a * a.one_like() == a` - **Inverse**: for non-zero `a`, `a * a.inv().unwrap() == a.one_like()` - **Division consistency**: for non-zero `b`, `a / b == a * b.inv().unwrap()` ### Ring axioms - **Distributivity**: `a * (b + c) == a * b + a * c` - **Zero annihilation**: `a * a.zero_like() == a.zero_like()` ### Characteristic - Sum of `p` copies of the multiplicative identity equals zero: `one + one + ... + one (p times) == zero` ### Hash consistency - `a == b` implies `hash(a) == hash(b)` ### ConstField-specific (when `T: ConstField`) - `T::zero().is_zero() == true` - `T::one().is_one() == true` - `T::order()` equals `p^m` (characteristic^extension_degree) ## Design ```rust /// Run the full field axiom test suite for the given field type. /// Elements are generated by the provided proptest strategy. pub fn test_field_axioms( strategy: impl Strategy, ) { // Each axiom is a separate proptest with clear failure messages } /// Additional tests for ConstField types. pub fn test_const_field_axioms( strategy: impl Strategy, ) { test_field_axioms(strategy.clone()); // Plus ConstField-specific tests } ``` ## Cross-implementation verification For field types that have both naive and optimized implementations (e.g., naive `Fp

` vs Montgomery `Fp

`), add cross-verification tests: - For random inputs, both implementations produce identical results for all operations. - For SIMD vs scalar paths, both produce identical results. ## Field types to test initially | Type | Instances | `ConstField`? | |------|-----------|:---:| | `Gf2mElement` | GF(2^4), GF(2^8), GF(2^16) | No | | `Fp

` | Fp<2>, Fp<3>, Fp<5>, Fp<7>, Fp<65537>, Fp<2305843009213693951> (2^61-1) | Yes | ## Location `crates/gf2-core/src/field/axiom_tests.rs` (new file in the field module, `#[cfg(test)]`) ## Dependencies - bfe0ba7b (FiniteField trait) — the traits must exist before we can write generic tests against them ## Success criteria - Axiom harness is generic and reusable: adding a new field type requires only writing a `proptest::Strategy` and calling `test_field_axioms()`. - All axiom tests pass for `Gf2mElement` with the existing implementation (after Hash/Sub/Neg additions from 07d0a50e). - At least 1000 random cases per axiom per field instance.
